name: Kermit Ruffins
abstract: Kermit Ruffins (born December 19, 1964) is an American jazz trumpeter, singer, and composer from New Orleans. He has been influenced by Louis Armstrong and Louis Jordan and says that the highest note he can hit on trumpet is a high C.

about_quotes.speaker: David Torkanowsky
about_quotes.evidence: But like for instance Kermit Ruffins, you’ve heard of him? He can barely play the trumpet. But every note he plays sounds meaningful, and it draws you in. Because he means it.
about_quotes.doc_title: David Torkanowsky interviewed by Monk Rowe, New Hartford, New York, September 26, 2019

about_quotes.speaker: Wallace Davenport
about_quotes.evidence: Now you know a guy who's doing well and he plays trumpet and sings Kermit Ruffin. Right. Yeah, Kermit does the same thing.

about_quotes.speaker: Ellis Marsalis
about_quotes.evidence: He has performed and recorded with a diverse list of artists, including Al Hirt, Cannonball Adderley and Kermit Ruffins.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ellis Marsalis interviewed by Monk Rowe, New Orleans, Louisiana, January 7, 2020
